title: Quantum-Classical Equivalence for AND-Functions
authors: Sreejata Kishor Bhattacharya, Farzan Byramji, Arkadev Chattopadhyay, Yogesh Dahiya, Shachar Lovett
abstract: A major open problem in quantum communication complexity is whether quantum protocols can be exponentially more efficient than classical protocols for computing total Boolean functions; the prevailing conjecture is that they cannot be so. In a seminal work, Razborov (2002) resolved this question for AND-functions of the form $$ F(x,y) = f(x_1 \land y_1, \ldots, x_n \land y_n), $$ when the outer function $f$ is symmetric, by proving that their bounded-error quantum and classical communication complexities are polynomially related. Since then, extending this result to all AND-functions has remained open and has been posed by several authors.   In this work, we settle this problem in a strong way. We show that for every Boolean function $f$, the bounded-error quantum and classical deterministic communication complexities of the function $f \circ \mathrm{AND}_2$ are polynomially related, up to polylogarithmic factors in $n$. We prove this by showing that both are characterized--up to polynomial loss--by the logarithm of the De Morgan sparsity of $f$.   Our results build on the recent work of Chattopadhyay, Dahiya, and Lovett (2025) on structural characterizations of non-sparse Boolean functions, which we extend to resolve the conjecture for general AND-functions.
summary: This work resolves a major open problem in quantum communication complexity by proving that for AND-functions, the quantum and classical deterministic communication complexities are polynomially equivalent. The authors achieve this by deeply connecting these complexity measures to the structural property of De Morgan sparsity of the function. This result significantly narrows the gap between quantum and classical computational power for this important class of functions.
main_problem: The paper addresses the open problem of whether quantum protocols can provide an exponential advantage over classical protocols for computing general total Boolean functions.
main_result: It proves that for the specific class of AND-functions, the bounded-error quantum and classical deterministic communication complexities are polynomially related, up to polylogarithmic factors.
method: The proof relies on structural characterizations of Boolean functions, relating communication complexity bounds to the function's De Morgan sparsity and using advanced Fourier analysis techniques on random restrictions.
model_or_system: The model is the two-party communication complexity setting (Alice and Bob) for computing total Boolean functions, specifically focusing on functions composed with the AND-2 gadget ($f \circ 	ext{AND}_2$).
key_observables: Deterministic communication complexity ($D_{ccp}$), bounded-error quantum communication complexity ($Q_{ccp}$), and the De Morgan sparsity ($	ext{sparf}$) of the outer function $f$.
important_parameters: The polynomial relationship holds up to polylogarithmic factors in $n$, and the lower bounds are derived from the sparsity $s$ of the function $f$.
main_assumptions: The equivalence is proven for AND-functions, not for all total Boolean functions (the general Log-Equivalence Conjecture remains open).
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the construction of semi-adaptive max-degree restriction trees for specific functions like $	ext{And}^3 \oplus 	ext{Or}^2$ and $	ext{Orn}$, demonstrating the structural machinery used in the proof.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by first establishing the general framework of communication complexity, then developing tools based on sparsity and restriction trees, and finally applying these tools to prove the polynomial equivalence for the AND-function class.
why_interesting: While focused on classical communication complexity, the techniques involving Fourier analysis, restriction/averaging procedures, and structural characterizations of functions are highly relevant to understanding the complexity of quantum algorithms and quantum information processing.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: A semi-adaptive max-degree restriction tree for And3 ˝ Or2 with core variables V “ tx1, x2, x3u. Leaves correspond to the 2|V | restrictions in the tree. For each leaf restriction, the re- stricted function pAnd3 ˝ Or2q|ρ computes an And over exactly the variables left free.
